description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who has been asked to formalise “policy-as-code” around the handling of environment variables and dotenv files for a small internal service.  
    Carry out the steps below entirely from the Linux terminal (no elevated privileges).  
    When you finish, the filesystem and the active shell session must be in the exact state described.

    1.  Create a new workspace directory  
        /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo  

    2.  Inside that directory create the following three files **with precisely the shown names and byte-for-byte contents**.

        2.1  /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/.env.template  
              ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
              │DB_USER=                                         │
              │DB_PASSWORD=                                     │
              │API_TOKEN=                                       │
              │LOG_LEVEL=INFO                                   │
              └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

        2.2  /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/.env.secure  
              (exact order and no extra whitespace)
              ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
              │DB_USER=backend_service                         │
              │DB_PASSWORD=P@55w0rd!23                         │
              │API_TOKEN=67f21f8a-9e9c-4c88-b0f2-3ab5e3bdab60   │
              │LOG_LEVEL=INFO                                  │
              └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

        2.3  /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/load_env.sh  
              The bash script must do ALL of the following:  
                • Read key=value pairs from “.env.secure”.  
                • Export each pair into the **current** shell environment.  
                • Create (if missing) or append (if it already exists) a log file  
                  /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/logs/execution.log  
                  Each successful export must add exactly one line to the log using the format  
                    [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] Exported <VAR_NAME>  
                  where the timestamp is the current local time; never write the secret values.  
                • Never echo the raw secrets to STDOUT or STDERR.  
              Note: the script itself must be POSIX-compliant bash and be given execute permission (mode 0755).

    3.  Make sure a directory /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/logs exists before the script runs.

    4.  Establish basic VCS hygiene: create a .gitignore file at  
        /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/.gitignore  
        that contains **exactly** the two lines below (and nothing else):  
            .env.secure  
            logs/  

    5.  Source the script (not merely run it in a subshell) so that the four variables
        DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, API_TOKEN and LOG_LEVEL are available to the current shell.  

    6.  Verification artefact – environment dump  
        After sourcing, capture the values in a file  
        /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/env_after_source.log  
        with the following **strict** format (4 lines, nothing more, nothing less):  
            DB_USER=<value>  
            DB_PASSWORD=<value>  
            API_TOKEN=<value>  
            LOG_LEVEL=<value>  

        This file will be read by the automated grader to ensure the variables
        were exported correctly.

    7.  The grader will perform these checks:

        • /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo exists.  
        • The three files in step 2 match exactly (byte for byte, including new-lines).  
        • /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/.gitignore contains the two required lines and no others.  
        • /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/logs/execution.log exists and contains at least four lines,
          each matching the regular expression  
              ^\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\] Exported (DB_USER|DB_PASSWORD|API_TOKEN|LOG_LEVEL)$  
        • The script load_env.sh is executable (mode 0755).  
        • When the grader executes “source /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/load_env.sh” in a fresh shell,
          subsequent calls to “printenv” return the exact secret values specified in .env.secure.  
        • /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/env_after_source.log exists and holds the four key=value lines identical
          to the .env.secure contents (same order, no trailing spaces).

    8.  Do not hard-code absolute timestamps in any file you create; they must be generated at runtime.

    9.  Apart from the files explicitly mentioned above, do not create or modify anything else in /home/user
        (the grader may fail the submission if unexpected artefacts are found).

    Summarised deliverables (all relative to /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo):

        .env.template           (exact placeholder template)
        .env.secure             (actual secrets)
        load_env.sh             (executable policy-as-code script)
        .gitignore              (lists secrets & logs)
        logs/execution.log      (runtime audit trail)
        env_after_source.log    (export verification snapshot)

    Follow best DevSecOps practices: never reveal the secret values on STDOUT; only the env_after_source.log file should contain them.
truth:
Directory that must exist before grading:
        /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo

    Files whose exact contents are mandated (byte-for-byte):

        1. /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/.env.template
           DB_USER=
           DB_PASSWORD=
           API_TOKEN=
           LOG_LEVEL=INFO

        2. /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/.env.secure
           DB_USER=backend_service
           DB_PASSWORD=P@55w0rd!23
           API_TOKEN=67f21f8a-9e9c-4c88-b0f2-3ab5e3bdab60
           LOG_LEVEL=INFO

        3. /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/.gitignore
           .env.secure
           logs/

    The grader will dynamically verify /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/load_env.sh behaves as demanded:
        • Sourcing it exports the four variables with the following exact values:
              DB_USER=backend_service
              DB_PASSWORD=P@55w0rd!23
              API_TOKEN=67f21f8a-9e9c-4c88-b0f2-3ab5e3bdab60
              LOG_LEVEL=INFO
        • It creates/updates /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/logs/execution.log,
          appending one audit line per variable in the
          “[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] Exported VAR” format (no secrets).

    Additional required artefact generated by the agent:
        /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo/env_after_source.log
        with exactly:
            DB_USER=backend_service
            DB_PASSWORD=P@55w0rd!23
            API_TOKEN=67f21f8a-9e9c-4c88-b0f2-3ab5e3bdab60
            LOG_LEVEL=INFO

    Permissions:
        load_env.sh must be 0755.
        All other new files default to 0644 (the grader will not fail if umask differs, provided read rights are intact).

    The grader will also ensure that no additional unexpected files or directories are present inside /home/user/devsecops_policy_demo.
